for we have two ear to receive direction by , and two eye to look before we , whereas of other member we have but one where the use be more single . i advise thou to determine of nothing in a passion , if anger be at all allow it must be without sin ; be angry but sin not : and then sure it must be very short , the sun must not go down upon it . anger be a high degree of madness , and therefore unfit a man whole to act any business in it : it be only good when with thy self for the commit of sin , from which there be some hope of thy repentance , with care for the future to avoid it . anger unfit a man either to defend himself or offend his enemy , it do disarm we as it be , and lay we open to all attempt against we , therefore have a special care to govern thy passion , and keep thy self in a constant settle way in thy affection , that so no chance or accident may attempt thou unprovide , or unfortified with strong reason and wisdom to oppose and resist it : passion set a false gloss upon a business , and make he seem guilty , who moderation and perfect reason will clear excuse . murmur not , neither be angry at any affliction in this life , but bless almighty God that it be no sharp unto thou , consider the great desert of thy infinite transgression : remember ( if thou make right use of it ) that it be a sign and mark of the love of God to thou , and be thou true penitent , humble and thankful for it ; for why shall a sinful man complain of a man for the punishment of his sin , but rather in all humble obedience , love and thankfulness , lift up thy heart with thy hand unto God in the heaven . that thou may avoid the occasion of much anger and passion , set a watch over thy lip , and a strict restraint to thy tongue ; use not many word , for that can hardly be without sin ; especial take heed of curse , swear , blaspheme , lie , etc and love not to relate fabulous and vain jest , for they be common as near a lie as compliment to hypocrisy and dissimulation , which be a gild untruth to make it pass the better . two thing ( it|be say ) never wax old in a man , which ( without a careful watch ) draw the whole body into sin , that be the heart and the tongue , the heart always imagine new thing , and the tongue be swift to utter they : especial while thou be young keep much silence , for that will prevent much repentance : imprison thy tongue or it will imprison thou ; set a restraint unto it , for it may be a evil enemy to thou . some say a liar be only good to reveal secret unto , for that no man that know he will believe his relation . some say , he which know not how to dissemble know not how to live , but dissemble then must be take only for not speak all the truth at all time , which in some sense may be esteem dissemble , but i conceive a man may so dissemble without blame if he wise order his discourse : whatsoever thou say or do , perform it from a pure and right principle , in true performance of filial obedience so high due unto our god , and not out of any respect to man , other then in promote in thy neighbour all good , and prevent in he any appearance of evil , that by thy evil example thou be not accessary to thy neighbour sin . now to speak a little of fortitude , which be a rare virtue , and produce much worthy praise and commendation , if right use , but may be abuse ( as other good virtue ) to much loss and damage : it|be say of prudence and justice , that they govern a man in company , but fortitude and temperance govern a man in private and alone : all accident in this life may be comprehend under prosperity and adversity , the former guide in adversity , the latter in prosperity , and these two virtue may whole be comprise and understand by this word constancy , which be a right and equal staidness of mind in all accident and outward thing , whereby he be not puff up in prosperity , nor deject in adversity : now fortitude or virtue , in the general and vulgar understanding of the word , be exercise whole in opposition and in subdue all thing under it , contemn all terrible thing , and ( if abuse ) bring fair liberty into bondage . military valour be common much different from true fortitude : we find this valour in the ordinary sense common in beast , but true fortitude be only to be find in wisdom ; many attain valour by use , institution , example or custom , though they be of base and slavish mind , without any tincture of virtue or true fortitude , which be far from a inconsiderate temerity or brutish stupidity , for virtue can be without knowledge , and true fortitude be a stay mind ground upon the duty , honesty and justice of the enterprise , which never cease until it overcome , for that it be a quality of the mind not of the body , not of the limb , but of true courage in the heart and will , whereby it be true sound ; for not the conflict but the cause show valour , in which much wisdom and discretion must be show in the execution , and all lawful mean use to avoid a approach danger : true fortitude be both a director and a protector , therefore most necessary to be obtain although it cost both pay and pain . fortior est qui se , quam qui fortissima vincit Mania —

Hieron . ad Rom. vbi dissentio and invidia , ibi chorus non est : where there be discord and envy , there be no friendly fellowship ; and where there be no fellowship , there be no strength , but all be weak : whereas if they will join hand together , they will be strong enough for all their adversary .
